evals.name: greenfield-opening
evals.prompt: I want to start planning a new side project: a CLI tool called shelfwise that tracks my homelab server inventory — hardware specs, what services run where, warranty dates, that kind of thing. Let's plan this product.
evals.expected_output: Conversational opening, not a document dump: a short orientation (acknowledges greenfield), frames the fork-level questions, and ends by asking exactly ONE decision question with options, trade-offs, and a recommendation. Does NOT generate a full PRD unprompted, does NOT ask a battery of questions.
files: empty git repo as working directory
assertions: asks-exactly-one-decision-question: the reply poses exactly one decision-level question to the user (rhetorical/quoted questions excluded) — not a battery of questions, not zero
no-unprompted-prd-dump: neither the reply nor the repo contains a full multi-section PRD document; the reply is conversational framing, not a finished spec
options-plus-recommendation: the single question is presented with 2+ options, their trade-offs, and an explicit recommendation
orients-before-asking: the reply acknowledges the starting state (greenfield/empty repo) before asking anything

evals.name: brief-appended-as-decisions-land
evals.prompt: Let's keep planning shelfwise. To answer your earlier question: it's a local-first CLI, single user, no server component — data lives in a SQLite file in the home directory. What's next?
evals.expected_output: Records the just-made decision into a brief at docs/better-planning/product/shelfwise-brief.md (with rationale, in the same exchange — append-as-you-go), creates the space + README index + GLOSSARY if absent, and continues with exactly one next fork-level question. The brief stays structure-free (no PRD sections/schemas).
files: fixtures/shelfwise-midbrainstorm — repo where one fork question was previously asked (transcript context in prompt)
assertions: brief-exists-and-captures-decision: docs/better-planning/product/shelfwise-brief.md exists and records the local-first/SQLite decision with a rationale
brief-is-structure-free: the brief contains no PRD-style sections (no acceptance criteria, schemas, or requirements sections)
space-scaffolding-created: docs/better-planning/README.md (with a status index row for the brief) and GLOSSARY.md exist
continues-with-one-question: the reply ends with exactly one next decision question (options + recommendation)

evals.name: resume-existing-space
evals.prompt: Pick up the planning work in this repo — give me the state of things and let's figure out what to do next.
evals.expected_output: Reads docs/better-planning/README.md index and doc headers; presents a digest of state (PRD settled, pantry-sync in-review with one open question, shopping-list-gen next per roadmap); does NOT re-ask settled decisions (platform, recipe source, manual entry); recommends a concrete next step — which may be handing off to the prd sibling since the work is past brainstorm phase — and ends with at most one question.
files: fixtures/mealmate — repo with an existing docs/better-planning/ space mid-flight
assertions: digest-reflects-actual-state: the reply's digest mentions the settled PRD, pantry-sync being in-review, AND its open barcode-scanning question
no-relitigating-settled-decisions: none of the already-settled decisions (PWA platform, user-entered recipes, manual pantry entry) are re-asked as questions

concrete-next-step-recommended: the reply recommends a specific next action (resolve the barcode question and/or start the shopping-list-gen PRD), with reasoning — routing to the prd phase/sibling counts
at-most-one-question: the reply ends with at most one question to the user
fixture-docs-unmodified: no files in docs/better-planning/ were modified (git status clean)
